human_verification.notes: Reviewed hospital case-management planning boundary, EHR/ADT/order/documentation/discharge/transition/utilization/authorization/post-acute/coding/claim evidence expectations, PHI handling, clinical and payer escalation rules, source authority mapping, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required healthcare-and-care-delivery research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for clinical judgment, discharge readiness, medical necessity, utilization review determinations, payer interpretation, authorization approval, medical coding, claim disposition, PHI disclosure, legal, financial approval, staffing approval, and patient-safety decisions.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers normal allocation, demand over capacity, priority conflict, final approval refusal, missing prerequisites, PHI handling, clinical escalation, payer/utilization boundary refusal, documentation rework, patient safety, source conflict, tenant terminology, adjacent specialist routing, and system access blockers.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, evidence, and approval event.
